use_case: Organic search keyword rankings and click performance from Google Search Console. Used for SEO analysis
granularity: One row per search query keyword per day

fields.name: query
fields.description: The search query keyword typed by the user

fields.name: date
fields.description: Date of the search impressions and clicks

fields.name: clicks
fields.description: Number of clicks from search results

fields.name: impressions
fields.description: Number of times the URL appeared in search results

fields.name: position
fields.description: Average ranking position in search results (lower is better)

fields.name: rank_by_impressions
fields.description: Rank of this keyword by impression volume on this date (1 = highest impressions)

fields.name: rank_by_clicks
fields.description: Rank of this keyword by click volume on this date (1 = highest clicks)

fields.name: capsuite_ref
fields.description: Client identifier slug used to partition blobs in Azure Blob Storage

use_case: Stores the connection state and credentials config for each external data source integrated with the CDP. One row per integration type. Used to check whether a source is connected, when it was last synced, and whether errors occurred.
granularity: One row per integration_type (googleAnalytics, googleSearchConsole, shopify, shopifyCustomApp, wordpress)

fields.name: created_date
fields.description: Timestamp when this integration record was first created

fields.name: updated_date
fields.description: Timestamp of the last update to this record, auto-maintained by trigger

fields.name: created_by
fields.description: ID of the app.users member who connected this integration. Null for records created before creator attribution existed or when the creating user has been deleted (ON DELETE SET NULL).

fields.name: integration_type
fields.description: Identifier for the integration. One of: googleAnalytics, googleSearchConsole, shopify, shopifyCustomApp, wordpress

fields.name: config
fields.description: Credential and configuration payload specific to the integration type. For googleAnalytics: {propertyId, propertyName}. For googleSearchConsole: {siteUrl}. For shopify: {storeName, accessToken}. For shopifyCustomApp: {storeName, scriptTagId, accessToken}.

fields.name: is_connected
fields.description: True when the integration has been successfully verified as connected

fields.name: last_connected_date
fields.description: Timestamp of the most recent successful connection test

fields.name: is_connection_error
fields.description: True when the last connection attempt failed

fields.name: connection_error
fields.description: Error message from the last failed connection attempt, null if no error

fields.name: is_synced
fields.description: True when a data sync has completed successfully at least once

fields.name: last_synced_date
fields.description: Timestamp of the most recent successful data sync

fields.name: is_sync_error
fields.description: True when the last sync attempt failed

fields.name: sync_error
fields.description: Error message from the last failed sync, null if no error

use_case: Single-row configuration table that controls which CDP and GA report pipelines are active and in debug mode. Also stores the GA property URL (url_domain) used to tie web content scraping to the correct domain. Updated automatically when Google Analytics is connected or disconnected.
granularity: Single configuration row per tenant (single-tenant deployment)

fields.name: created_date
fields.description: Timestamp when this config record was created

fields.name: updated_date
fields.description: Timestamp of the last update, auto-maintained by trigger

fields.name: created_by
fields.description: ID of the app.users member who created/last owns this config row. Null for the system-seeded default row or when the creating user has been deleted (ON DELETE SET NULL).

fields.name: capsuite_ref
fields.description: Client reference slug used to namespace data across pipeline jobs

fields.name: is_trial
fields.description: True when the client is on a trial plan; may affect data retention and pipeline limits

fields.name: url_domain
fields.description: The GA4 property URL (propertyName) set when Google Analytics is connected. Used as the domain identifier for web content and report configuration. Cleared when GA is disconnected.

fields.name: supporting_capsuite_param
fields.description: List of Capsuite tracking parameter names injected into the site. Defaults to [capsuite_sid, capsuite_apid].

fields.name: cdp_reports
fields.description: Per-report debug and configuration flags for CDP pipeline reports. Keys: popup_tracking, blast_tracking, utm_summary, activity_log, web_content_attributes, ap_session_log, ap_anonymous_profile, ap_segment_filter, mem_segment_filter, ap_membership_profiles, outbound_links_attributes. Each entry has isDebugging (bool) and debugStartDate (YYYY-MM-DD).

fields.name: ga_reports
fields.description: Per-cube debug/config flags for the GA data pipeline (one key per enabled cube in cube_catalog, grouped by the purpose DAGs path_funnel/content/purchase/ecommerce/acquisition/audience/retention). Example keys: path_exploration, page_engagement_daily, funnel_report, page_metrics, website_metrics, event_list, purchase_list, acquisition_session_daily, channel_daily, landing_page_daily, geo_daily, tech_daily, demographics_daily, returning_daily, transaction_metrics, item_performance, cohort_weekly. Each entry has isDebugging (bool) and debugStartDate (YYYY-MM-DD). The retired flat utm_*/country_performance tables were dropped.

use_case: Configuration for the web content scraper that crawls the client's website to extract page attributes. url_pattern is derived from the GA property URL and is used by the scraper to identify pages belonging to this client. Updated automatically when Google Analytics is connected or disconnected.
granularity: Single configuration row per tenant (single-tenant deployment)

fields.name: created_date
fields.description: Timestamp when this config record was created

fields.name: updated_date
fields.description: Timestamp of the last update, auto-maintained by trigger

fields.name: created_by
fields.description: ID of the app.users member who created/last owns this scraper config row. Null for the system-seeded default row or when the creating user has been deleted (ON DELETE SET NULL).

fields.name: capsuite_ref
fields.description: Client reference slug used to namespace data across pipeline jobs

fields.name: cut_off_point_after
fields.description: HTML marker string; the scraper discards page content that appears after this string. Null means no upper cutoff.

fields.name: cut_off_point_before
fields.description: HTML marker string; the scraper discards page content that appears before this string. Null means no lower cutoff.

fields.name: update_time_elements
fields.description: Meta tag name used to extract page last-updated timestamps (e.g. og:updated_time)

fields.name: error_strings
fields.description: List of strings that indicate a scraped page is an error page (404, 403, etc.). Pages containing any of these strings are discarded. Defaults include 'Error 404', 'ERROR 403', 'seem to exist', and CJK equivalents.

fields.name: valid_content_min_length
fields.description: Minimum character length for extracted page content to be considered valid. Pages with fewer characters are discarded. Default is 60.

fields.name: url_pattern
fields.description: Subdomain slug extracted from the GA property URL, used to identify pages belonging to this client during scraping. For example, propertyName 'https://www.mrmiles.hk' produces url_pattern 'mrmiles'. Set automatically on GA connect, cleared on GA disconnect.

use_case: Core multi-tenant entity. Each company is an isolated workspace. Created at user registration or via the Companies API. Automatically linked to the interaction-service microservice at creation time so popup targeting and analytics work from day one.
granularity: One row per company (workspace)

fields.name: id
fields.description: Primary key, auto-generated UUID. This is the CDP company ID shown in the WordPress plugin integration panel.

fields.name: created_date
fields.description: Timestamp when the company was created (i.e. when the first user registered).

fields.name: updated_date
fields.description: Timestamp of the last update, auto-maintained by trigger.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Display name of the company, entered at registration.

fields.name: slug
fields.description: URL-safe unique identifier derived from the company name. Used in API paths.

fields.name: logo_url
fields.description: Optional company logo URL shown in the sidebar.

fields.name: plan_expires_at
fields.description: When the current plan expires. Null for free tier.

fields.name: is_active
fields.description: Whether the company workspace is active. Setting false blocks all access.

fields.name: interaction_service_company_id
fields.description: UUID assigned by the interaction-service microservice when the company is registered. Set automatically at company creation (registration, Google OAuth, or Companies API). Used as the company reference for all popup targeting, activity tracking, and analytics queries against the interaction service. Null only for companies created before this feature was deployed (will be back-filled on first popup operation).

fields.name: interaction_service_synced_at
fields.description: Timestamp of when the company was last successfully registered or synced with the interaction service.

fields.name: settings
fields.description: Freeform key-value settings for the company. Previously stored interaction_service_company_id here; now uses the dedicated column instead.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Additional metadata for the company, reserved for future use.

use_case: Monthly conversion funnel (Session start -> Page view -> Scroll -> Purchase), broken down by source/medium and by campaign. Use for drop-off and funnel-completion analysis.
granularity: One row per funnel step per breakdown (source_medium or campaign) per month

fields.name: funnel_step_name
fields.description: Step label: Session start, Page view, Page scroll, Purchase

fields.name: funnel_step_abandonments
fields.description: Users who abandoned at this step

use_case: Unified eCommerce customers synced from the connected store platforms (Shopify today). Source feed for app.customer_profiles - prefer customer_profiles for audience analytics; query this for raw store-level customer detail
granularity: One row per store customer per platform. customer_id = {capsuite_ref}_cust_{nativeId}

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Workspace (tenant) id - ALWAYS filter by this

fields.name: source_platform
fields.description: Origin platform of the row: shopify | shopline | odoo | manual (user CSV import)

fields.name: source_id
fields.description: Native id of the record in the source platform

fields.name: source_extra
fields.description: Platform-specific extra fields not in the neutral shape (NULL when none)

fields.name: customer_id
fields.description: Unified customer id; equals app.customer_profiles.member_id for synced members

fields.name: customer_no
fields.description: Customer number in the source system (may be null)

fields.name: join_date
fields.description: When the customer was created in the store

fields.name: is_company
fields.description: True when the customer is a company (B2B platforms)

fields.name: has_email
fields.description: Customer has an email on file

fields.name: has_phone
fields.description: Customer has a phone on file

use_case: Unified eCommerce order headers across platforms. Revenue, AOV, repeat-purchase and customer-lifetime analyses. Rows with source_platform='manual' are orders the user CSV-imported themselves - treat them exactly like synced store orders. NOTE: 'order' is a reserved word - always write commerce."order" (quoted) in SQL
granularity: One row per order per platform. order_id = {capsuite_ref}_order_{nativeId}

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Workspace (tenant) id - ALWAYS filter by this

fields.name: source_platform
fields.description: Origin platform of the row: shopify | shopline | odoo | manual (user CSV import)

fields.name: source_id
fields.description: Native id of the record in the source platform

fields.name: source_extra
fields.description: Platform-specific extra fields not in the neutral shape (NULL when none)

fields.name: customer_id
fields.description: Unified customer id (joins commerce.customer / app.customer_profiles.member_id); NULL for guest checkout

fields.name: net_amount
fields.description: Current order total in the order currency

fields.name: exchange_rate
fields.description: Exchange rate to base currency (1 when same)

fields.name: order_status
fields.description: Unified status: draft | confirmed | completed | cancelled. Use IN ('completed','confirmed') for real revenue

fields.name: total_refunded_amt
fields.description: Total amount refunded on this order

use_case: Unified order line items (products + shipping lines) across platforms. Product/basket analyses, top sellers, category mix
granularity: One row per order line per platform. order_line_id = {capsuite_ref}_orderline_{nativeId}

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Workspace (tenant) id - ALWAYS filter by this

fields.name: source_platform
fields.description: Origin platform of the row: shopify | shopline | odoo | manual (user CSV import)

fields.name: source_id
fields.description: Native id of the record in the source platform

fields.name: source_extra
fields.description: Platform-specific extra fields not in the neutral shape (NULL when none)

fields.name: customer_id
fields.description: Unified customer id of the order

fields.name: product_id
fields.description: Unified product id at variant grain (joins commerce.product)

fields.name: product_type
fields.description: Product type/category from the product master

fields.name: bundle_id
fields.description: Bundle id when the line belongs to a bundle

use_case: Unified product master across platforms, at VARIANT grain. Catalog size, price-point and category analyses; join target for order_line.product_id
granularity: One row per product variant per platform. product_id = {capsuite_ref}_product_{nativeVariantId}

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Workspace (tenant) id - ALWAYS filter by this

fields.name: source_platform
fields.description: Origin platform of the row: shopify | shopline | odoo | manual (user CSV import)

fields.name: source_id
fields.description: Native id of the record in the source platform

fields.name: source_extra
fields.description: Platform-specific extra fields not in the neutral shape (NULL when none)

use_case: Order-level refunds across platforms. Refund-rate and revenue-quality analyses (pairs with commerce."order".total_refunded_amt)
granularity: One row per refund per platform. refund_id = {capsuite_ref}_refund_{nativeId}

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Workspace (tenant) id - ALWAYS filter by this

fields.name: source_platform
fields.description: Origin platform of the row: shopify | shopline | odoo | manual (user CSV import)

fields.name: source_id
fields.description: Native id of the record in the source platform

fields.name: source_extra
fields.description: Platform-specific extra fields not in the neutral shape (NULL when none)

use_case: Refund line-item detail (which products were refunded/restocked). Product-level return-rate analyses
granularity: One row per refunded line item per platform

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Workspace (tenant) id - ALWAYS filter by this

fields.name: source_platform
fields.description: Origin platform of the row: shopify | shopline | odoo | manual (user CSV import)

fields.name: source_id
fields.description: Native id of the record in the source platform

fields.name: source_extra
fields.description: Platform-specific extra fields not in the neutral shape (NULL when none)

use_case: Current stock snapshot per product variant per location across platforms (fully replaced on every sync - a snapshot, not history)
granularity: One row per (product variant, location) per platform. inventory_level_id = {product_id}_{location_id}

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Workspace (tenant) id - ALWAYS filter by this

fields.name: source_platform
fields.description: Origin platform of the row: shopify | shopline | odoo | manual (user CSV import)

fields.name: source_id
fields.description: Native id of the record in the source platform

fields.name: source_extra
fields.description: Platform-specific extra fields not in the neutral shape (NULL when none)

fields.name: quantity_reserved
fields.description: Reserved quantity (platforms that report it; NULL otherwise)

fields.name: snapshot_date
fields.description: When this level was last reported

use_case: app.customer_profiles - the unified golden-record member list. One row per resolved person per workspace (company_id), stitched from every membership source (manual.membership + commerce.customer). USE THIS for all audience analytics: it pre-aggregates each member's GA web activity, commerce spend, and offline events so you rarely need to join. ALWAYS filter by company_id.
granularity: One row per resolved person per workspace (PK: company_id, member_id)

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Workspace id. ALWAYS filter by this.

fields.name: member_id
fields.description: Resolved person key, unique per company (part of PK).

fields.name: member_source
fields.description: Where the primary record came from: manual | shopify | ga | mixed.

fields.name: created_date
fields.description: When this profile row was created.

fields.name: updated_date
fields.description: When this profile row was last updated.

fields.name: primary_email
fields.description: Primary email. 'email eligible' = is_opt_in_email = true AND primary_email IS NOT NULL.

fields.name: member_join_date
fields.description: When the person became a member. Use for cohort/tenure/registration analyses.

fields.name: member_last_update
fields.description: Last time the membership record changed.

fields.name: member_reg_channel
fields.description: Registration channel - use for channel attribution.

fields.name: is_company
fields.description: True if the member is a company/organisation rather than a person.

fields.name: has_email
fields.description: Whether a usable email is on file.

fields.name: has_phone
fields.description: Whether a usable phone is on file.

fields.name: is_opt_in_email
fields.description: Email marketing consent. Allowed for segmentation; NOT for planning email sends (email is a coming-soon feature).

fields.name: ga_sessions
fields.description: Matched GA sessions. 0 = no web activity; > 0 = has web activity; >= 5 = highly active.

fields.name: ga_last_seen
fields.description: Most recent GA activity date. Good proxy for last web activity.

fields.name: ga_top_source_medium
fields.description: Most common traffic source/medium, e.g. 'google / cpc'.

fields.name: ga_visitor_ids
fields.description: GA anonymous ids (capsuite_apid) belonging to this member - the join key to ga_landing.path_exploration.

fields.name: ga_source_mediums
fields.description: All source/mediums seen for this member.

fields.name: ga_campaigns
fields.description: All campaigns seen for this member.

fields.name: ga_events_list
fields.description: Distinct GA events seen for this member.

fields.name: ga_pages_visited
fields.description: Distinct pages visited by this member.

fields.name: last_order_date
fields.description: Most recent order date. Good proxy for last purchase activity.

fields.name: seminar_count
fields.description: Number of seminar/event registrations. 0 = never attended; 'seminar attendee' = seminar_count > 0.

fields.name: seminars
fields.description: Array of offline events: {event_name, event_date, action}. Expand with jsonb_array_elements(seminars).

fields.name: attribute_count
fields.description: Number of custom attributes on the profile (display cache).

fields.name: attributes
fields.description: Custom attribute values (display cache; source of truth = app.profile_attribute_values).

fields.name: is_manual
fields.description: True if the record was manually created/uploaded.

fields.name: last_refreshed
fields.description: When the aggregated caches on this row were last rebuilt.

use_case: app.anonymous_profiles - unresolved web visitors (not yet linked to a known member). Built from ga_landing.path_exploration. Use for anonymous-audience segments (segment_type = 'anonymous_profile'). When an identity link appears (email capture / manual anonymous_id mapping) resolved_member_id is set and the visitor is linked to a customer_profiles row. ALWAYS filter by company_id.
granularity: One row per anonymous visitor per workspace (PK: company_id, visitor_id)

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Workspace id. ALWAYS filter by this.

fields.name: visitor_id
fields.description: GA anonymous id (capsuite_apid); part of PK.

fields.name: resolved_member_id
fields.description: Set once the visitor is stitched to a known member (else NULL).

fields.name: resolved_at
fields.description: When the visitor was resolved to a member.

fields.name: last_refreshed
fields.description: When this row's aggregates were last rebuilt.

use_case: app.profile_identities - the identity graph. Each row links a customer_profiles member to one contributing identity (an email, phone, source member_id, or GA anonymous_id). Use identity_type = 'anonymous_id' to join members to their GA anonymous ids (identity_value = capsuite_apid) for event-level GA analysis. ALWAYS filter by company_id.
granularity: One row per (member, identity) link per workspace

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Workspace id. ALWAYS filter by this.

fields.name: source
fields.description: Contributing source: manual | shopify | ga | interaction | popup.

fields.name: source_id
fields.description: Natural key in the source table.

fields.name: identity_type
fields.description: One of: email | phone | member_id | anonymous_id.

fields.name: identity_value
fields.description: The identity value (e.g. the email, phone, or capsuite_apid).

fields.name: is_primary
fields.description: Whether this is the member's primary identity of that type.

fields.name: first_seen
fields.description: When this identity was first linked.

fields.name: last_seen
fields.description: When this identity was last seen.

use_case: app.campaigns - UTM campaigns saved by users (the Campaigns/UTM page). Check this before suggesting a new UTM link to match the user's naming conventions and avoid duplicates. ALWAYS filter by company_id.
granularity: One row per saved UTM campaign

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Workspace id. ALWAYS filter by this.

fields.name: base_url
fields.description: Landing URL the UTM parameters are appended to.

use_case: app.segments - audience segments saved by users. segment_type = 'customer' targets app.customer_profiles; 'anonymous_profile' targets app.anonymous_profiles. estimated_size is the cached audience count. filter_criteria holds the rule tree (values may be scalar OR array). ALWAYS filter by company_id.
granularity: One row per saved segment

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Workspace id. ALWAYS filter by this.

fields.name: estimated_size
fields.description: Cached audience count (from the last preview/refresh).

fields.name: segment_type
fields.description: customer | anonymous_profile - which population it targets.

fields.name: daily_refresh
fields.description: Whether the size is refreshed daily.

fields.name: filter_criteria
fields.description: Rule tree defining membership; values may be scalar or array.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Extra metadata (e.g. cached criteria for popups).

use_case: manual.membership - CSV-uploaded member records (source of record for the manual source). The profile builder maps these into app.customer_profiles alongside commerce.customer, so for audience analytics prefer app.customer_profiles; query manual.membership directly only to inspect the raw uploaded rows. ALWAYS filter by company_id.
granularity: One row per uploaded member per workspace (PK: company_id, member_id)

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Workspace id. ALWAYS filter by this.

fields.name: anonymous_id
fields.description: Optional GA anonymous id supplied in the CSV - lets manual membership stitch to an anonymous web profile.

use_case: manual.sale - CSV-uploaded sales/order headers (manual source). Join to manual.sale_order_line on (company_id, trxn_id) for line items, or to a member via member_id. For per-member revenue prefer the pre-aggregated order_count / total_spend on app.customer_profiles. ALWAYS filter by company_id.
granularity: One row per uploaded transaction/order header (PK: company_id, trxn_id)

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Workspace id. ALWAYS filter by this.

fields.name: member_id
fields.description: → member (may be NULL for guest orders).

use_case: manual.sale_order_line - line items for manual.sale orders. Join to manual.sale on (company_id, trxn_id). Use for product-level revenue/quantity analysis on manually uploaded sales. ALWAYS filter by company_id.
granularity: One row per order line item (PK: company_id, trxn_item_id)

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Workspace id. ALWAYS filter by this.

use_case: manual.product - CSV-uploaded product catalogue (manual source). Reference for product names/categories/prices when analysing manual sales (manual.sale_order_line.prod_id). Distinct from commerce.product (synced-store catalogue). ALWAYS filter by company_id.
granularity: One row per uploaded product (PK: company_id, prod_id)

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Workspace id. ALWAYS filter by this.

use_case: Raw event stream (page/interaction events) with minute-level timing, referrer, location and last-touch acquisition - the base for path analysis and anonymous-profile rollups.
granularity: one row per event at minute granularity

fields.name: capsuite_uid
fields.description: Capsuite member/user id (links to a CRM member).

fields.name: capsuite_apid
fields.description: Capsuite anonymous profile id (web visitor cookie).

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Slimmed to page-level daily engagement duration (was a near-duplicate of path_exploration). Use for dwell/engagement per page; path_exploration holds the raw events.
granularity: date x page path (engagement seconds)

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Daily performance keyed by UTM id only.
granularity: date x utm_id

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Daily advertising cost/click metrics by campaign, with conversions and ROAS so ad spend can be judged on outcome (metrics only - no extra cardinality).
granularity: date x source x medium x campaign x utm_id, ad-cost + ROAS metrics

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Daily page-level metrics, with page title for analysis by page name/type rather than raw URL. NON-ADDITIVE: a user/session spans many pages, so user & session counts are NOT additive across rows (compare pages, don't sum; use website_metrics for site totals).
granularity: date x page path x page title

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Daily page x acquisition-channel metrics (converged from page x source x medium to page x channel_group to cut cardinality). NON-ADDITIVE: user counts are not additive across pages/channels.
granularity: date x page path x channel group

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Daily site-wide totals with derived engagement rates. Deduplicated baseline for reconciling wider cubes against the GA4 UI.
granularity: date (site-wide totals)

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Daily session-DEPTH metrics (pages/session, avg duration, bounce, engagement) by device - the session-level middle layer between events and site totals. Distinct from tech_daily, which is REACH (users/sessions) by device/OS/browser.
granularity: date x device (session depth / quality)

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Catalog of events seen per day and whether each is a key event.
granularity: date x event name x is_key_event

fields.name: is_key_event
fields.description: Whether the event is a key event.

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: GA purchase events keyed by transaction id plus capsuite identity keys. IDENTITY JOIN POINT ONLY - it carries WHO purchased (link to member/order), NOT the order value; revenue comes from commerce."order" via trxn_id, or from transaction_metrics for GA-side revenue by channel.
granularity: one row per purchase event (transaction id)

fields.name: capsuite_uid
fields.description: Capsuite member/user id (links to a CRM member).

fields.name: capsuite_apid
fields.description: Capsuite anonymous profile id (web visitor cookie).

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Daily product funnel (view -> cart -> purchase) and revenue per item; joins commerce.order_line on item_id.
granularity: date x item (dedicated item cube)

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Item revenue and units by acquisition channel (which channel drives which products). Overlaps item_performance on revenue/units but adds the channel split; item_performance is the full per-item funnel (views->cart->purchase).
granularity: date x item x channel group

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Daily transaction/revenue totals by channel; reconciles against commerce orders.
granularity: date x channel group

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Daily last-touch acquisition performance with conversions AND revenue - the campaign workhorse. Converged replacement for utm_daily_performance (one high-cardinality dim: campaign; outcome metrics add no cardinality).
granularity: date x source/medium x campaign (one HIGH dim)

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Daily FIRST-touch (user acquisition) performance - the first-touch counterpart to the session (last-touch) cube.
granularity: date x first-user source/medium x first-user channel

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Daily performance by default channel group; low-cardinality reconciliation cube closest to the GA4 'Traffic acquisition' overview.
granularity: date x channel group (low cardinality)

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Daily landing-page performance.
granularity: date x landing page (one HIGH dim)

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Daily audience by age/gender (needs Google Signals; rows may be thresholded).
granularity: date x age bracket x gender

fields.name: age_bracket
fields.description: User age bracket (needs Google Signals; thresholded).

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Daily performance by GA4 audience membership. NON-ADDITIVE: a user can belong to many audiences, so user/session counts overlap and must not be summed across audiences (compare, don't total).
granularity: date x audience name (one HIGH dim)

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Daily technographics REACH (users/sessions/engagement) by device/OS/browser. For session DEPTH (pages/session, duration) by device, use session_quality_daily.
granularity: date x device x OS x browser

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Daily performance by geography (country + region). Superset of country_performance.
granularity: date x country x region

fields.name: property_id
fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.

fields.name: company_id
fields.description: Tenant key (app.companies.id); ga_landing is partitioned by it.

use_case: Daily affinity/interest breakdown (verify brandingInterest availability per property). NON-ADDITIVE + thresholded: a user has many affinity categories, so counts heavily overlap (observed ~5-6x the deduplicated total) - use for relative interest popularity only, never sum.
granularity: date x interest (one HIGH dim)

fields.description: GA4 property id the row was fetched from.
